target.title: fix(gateway): guard reload metadata descriptors
target.body:
## Summary

- Hardens gateway config reload planning against malformed channel/plugin reload metadata.
- Keeps healthy channel/plugin reload rules usable when one descriptor is unreadable.
- Fails closed for unreadable plugin reload registrations on plugin control-plane config, so a bad descriptor cannot downgrade restart-required plugin state changes to generic hot reload.
- Out of scope: broad runtime/tool-schema doctor validation and full changed-gate proof, which is currently blocked by remote runner auth.

## Linked context

Related to the ongoing tool-schema/runtime fuzzing hardening sweep.

No linked issue.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A malformed channel/plugin reload descriptor can throw during gateway reload-plan construction and crash planning, or under-classify higher-priority restart/hot reload paths as no-op/hot when only part of the descriptor is readable.

Real environment tested: macOS gwt worktree, Node v24.15.0, pnpm 11.2.2.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-next179-finalrebase nodenv exec node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts --reporter=verbose`; `nodenv exec node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/config-reload-plan.ts src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`.

Evidence after fix: The focused gateway config reload shard passed with `Test Files 4 passed (4)` and `Tests 340 passed (340)`. Oxlint and diff-check exited cleanly. Branch autoreview reported `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.

Observed result after fix: `buildGatewayReloadPlan()` skips unreadable channel descriptors without crashing, preserves unrelated healthy reload rules, rejects lower-priority no-op rules when a higher-priority list cannot be read, and forces restart handling for affected plugin entry/control-plane paths when plugin reload registration metadata is unreadable.

What was not tested: Full `pnpm check:changed` did not run.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: `blacksmith testbox list --all` is blocked by missing Blacksmith auth. Direct AWS Crabbox proof is blocked by coordinator 401 on run history and lease creation.

Before evidence: Narrow red tests reproduced throws from `channel reload descriptor exploded`, `channel reload config prefixes exploded`, and `plugin reload registration exploded` before the guard/fail-closed changes.

## Tests and validation

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-next179-finalrebase nodenv exec node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `nodenv exec node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/config-reload-plan.ts src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- `blacksmith testbox list --all` blocked: not authenticated.
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run -provider aws -label next179-check-changed -shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` blocked: coordinator 401.

Regression coverage added:

- Unreadable channel `reload` descriptor getter.
- Unreadable channel reload prefix getter.
- Unreadable channel high-priority prefix getter with readable lower-priority no-op prefixes.
- Unreadable plugin reload registration getter.
- Unreadable plugin high-priority prefix getter with readable lower-priority no-op prefixes.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`

What is the highest-risk area? Reload classification could over- or under-restart gateway/plugin state.

How is that risk mitigated? Valid descriptors keep the existing ordering and behavior; malformed channel descriptors are skipped so unknown channel config falls back to restart; malformed plugin reload registrations add conservative restart rules for plugin control-plane paths instead of allowing the generic `plugins` hot rule to win.

## Current review state

What is the next action? Maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Full changed-gate proof is waiting on Testbox or Crabbox auth.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? Local autoreview findings about union-index type safety, lower-priority no-op leakage, and plugin restart-to-hot downgrade were fixed before push.

target.title: fix(agents): cap DSML recovery buffer to prevent unbounded memory growth
target.body:
Fixes #90015

## Summary

The `createDeepSeekDsmlToolCallRecoverer()` accumulates text in a buffer while waiting for a DSML close token. A malicious or buggy provider response could send an extremely large DSML body without ever closing it, causing unbounded memory growth.

Add a 256 KB cap (`MAX_DSML_RECOVERY_BUFFER_BYTES`) that discards the current DSML block as plain text when the buffer exceeds the limit. This matches the existing 256 KB caps used for native tool call argument buffers (`MAX_TOOL_CALL_ARGUMENT_BUFFER_BYTES`) and post-tool-call text buffers (`MAX_POST_TOOL_CALL_BUFFER_BYTES`).

## Changed files

- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts` -- add `MAX_DSML_RECOVERY_BUFFER_BYTES` constant and buffer overflow check in `createDeepSeekDsmlToolCallRecoverer()` when no close token is found
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` -- regression test: oversized DSML block with valid invoke markup is discarded, no tool calls recovered

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: The DSML recovery buffer in `createDeepSeekDsmlToolCallRecoverer()` grows without bound when a provider sends a DSML open token followed by a large body that never closes (or closes very late). This can cause memory pressure or OOM in the gateway process.

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5 arm64, Node v22.19.0, pnpm v11.2.2. Built from source at `/tmp/openclaw-86637`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

1. Built from branch at `/tmp/openclaw-86637`: `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
2. Ran focused DSML test suite: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts -t "DeepSeek DSML"`
3. Verified the new cap constant is consistent with existing caps: `grep -n MAX_DSML_RECOVERY_BUFFER_BYTES src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`

Evidence after fix: terminal output from the patched build:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts -t "DeepSeek DSML"

 RUN  v4.1.7 /private/tmp/openclaw-86637

 ✓ |agents-core| ../../src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts (241 tests | 234 skipped) 23ms
 ✓ |agents-support| ../../src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts (241 tests | 234 skipped) 22ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  14 passed | 468 skipped (482)
```

All 7 DSML tests pass per shard (14 total across 2 shards), including the new "discards oversized DeepSeek DSML recovery buffer as text" test.

```console
$ grep -n 'MAX_DSML_RECOVERY_BUFFER_BYTES\|MAX_TOOL_CALL_ARGUMENT_BUFFER\|MAX_POST_TOOL_CALL_BUFFER' src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts
2633:  const MAX_POST_TOOL_CALL_BUFFER_BYTES = 256_000;
2634:  const MAX_TOOL_CALL_ARGUMENT_BUFFER_BYTES = 256_000;
3097:const MAX_DSML_RECOVERY_BUFFER_BYTES = 256_000;
```

The new cap is consistent with the two existing 256 KB caps in the same file.

Observed result after fix: The `createDeepSeekDsmlToolCallRecoverer()` now discards oversized DSML blocks (>256 KB) as plain text instead of accumulating unbounded buffer memory. Existing DSML recovery (well-formed blocks, split chunks, malformed blocks, native tool call coexistence) is unchanged. The new cap at line 3097 matches the existing `MAX_TOOL_CALL_ARGUMENT_BUFFER_BYTES` and `MAX_POST_TOOL_CALL_BUFFER_BYTES` caps at lines 2633-2634.

What was not tested: Live DeepSeek model streaming with actual oversized DSML output (would require a DeepSeek API key and a provider that sends >256 KB DSML bodies). The test uses a synthetic 300 KB body streamed through the production `processOpenAICompletionsStream` function.

target.title: fix(tui): respect queue mode in sendMessage busy guard
target.body:
Fixes #90012

## Summary

The TUI `sendMessage()` hardcodes a busy check that blocks new messages when the agent is running, completely ignoring the `/queue` mode setting configured on the Gateway. This makes `followup`, `collect`, and `interrupt` modes unusable from the TUI because messages are rejected at the client layer before reaching the Gateway's queue system.

Two additional gaps existed:
1. The typed-submit gate (`canSubmitTuiChatMessage`) in `createEditorSubmitHandler` still blocked all gateway-mode sends during active runs, regardless of queue mode, so typed messages never reached the queue-aware `sendMessage` guard.
2. The session row only exposed `entry?.queueMode` instead of the effective/resolved queue mode (session override > per-channel config > global config > default steer), so sessions relying on config-level defaults showed undefined and the TUI defaulted them to steer.

## Changes

**Gateway session row** (`session-utils.types.ts`, `session-utils.ts`): Add `queueMode` to `GatewaySessionRow` and resolve it via `resolveQueueSettings` so the row exposes the effective queue mode (not just the raw entry field). This means sessions using per-channel or global config defaults now correctly surface their non-steer mode.

**TUI types** (`tui-types.ts`, `tui-backend.ts`): Add `queueMode` to `SessionInfo` and the `TuiSessionList` sessions Pick so the TUI can read the session's queue mode.

**TUI session propagation** (`tui-session-actions.ts`): Propagate `queueMode` from the session entry to `state.sessionInfo` during `applySessionInfo`.

**TUI busy guard** (`tui-command-handlers.ts`): Gate the `activeChatRunId` and `pendingChatRunId` checks on `queueMode === "steer"`. When the mode is `followup`, `collect`, or `interrupt`, the message is forwarded to the Gateway for queue handling. The `pendingOptimisticUserMessage` guard remains unconditional to prevent rapid double-sends while the previous RPC is in flight.

**Typed-submit gate** (`tui.ts`): Update `canSubmitTuiChatMessage` to accept and honor `queueMode`. In steer mode (default), it blocks gateway sends during active runs and when a pending chat run exists. In non-steer modes, only `pendingOptimisticUserMessage` blocks. The caller now passes `sessionInfo.queueMode` so the editor submit handler respects the queue mode. The blocked-message notification is also queue-mode-aware.

**Tests**: 13 new tests total: 7 for the queue-mode-aware `canSubmitTuiChatMessage` gate (followup/collect/interrupt allow, steer blocks, pendingOptimistic still blocks, pendingChatRunId allowed in followup), 3 for effective queue mode resolution in `buildGatewaySessionRow` (entry mode, global config fallback, default steer), and the original 3 for the command-handler busy guard + session propagation.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** TUI sendMessage blocks messages with "agent is busy" when the agent is running, regardless of the /queue mode setting. followup, collect, and interrupt modes never reach the Gateway. Additionally, the typed-submit path through `canSubmitTuiChatMessage` blocked messages before they reached the queue-aware sendMessage guard, and session rows only exposed the raw entry queueMode instead of the resolved effective mode.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 15.5, Node v22.19, OpenClaw built from patched source at `/tmp/wt-89078`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  Step 1. Run behavioral tests for both gates and session row:
  ```bash
  cd /tmp/wt-89078
  npm install
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui.config.ts \
    src/tui/tui.test.ts src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts \
    src/tui/tui-session-actions.test.ts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts
  ```

  Step 2. Run proof script exercising production code:
  ```bash
  npx tsx /tmp/proof-89078.ts
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:**

  All test suites pass (63 + 57 + 31 + 107 = 258 tests):

  ```console
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui.config.ts \
      src/tui/tui.test.ts src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts \
      src/tui/tui-session-actions.test.ts
   ✓ |tui| src/tui/tui.test.ts (63 tests) 24ms
   ✓ |tui| src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts (57 tests) 17ms
   ✓ |tui| src/tui/tui-session-actions.test.ts (31 tests) 12ms
   Test Files  3 passed (3)
        Tests  151 passed (151)

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts
   ✓ |gateway| src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts (107 tests)
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  107 passed (107)
  ```

  Production code proof with `canSubmitTuiChatMessage` and `resolveQueueSettings`:

  ```console
  $ npx tsx /tmp/proof-89078.ts

  === Fix 1: typed-submit gate respects queueMode ===

    PASS  steer blocks gateway active run: false
    PASS  explicit steer blocks gateway active run: false
    PASS  followup allows gateway active run: true
    PASS  collect allows gateway active run: true
    PASS  interrupt allows gateway active run: true
    PASS  followup blocks pendingOptimistic: false
    PASS  followup allows pendingChatRunId: true

  === Fix 2: effective queue mode resolution ===

    PASS  global config collect resolves to collect: collect
    PASS  entry interrupt overrides global collect: interrupt
    PASS  no config defaults to steer: steer
    PASS  per-channel discord followup resolves: followup

  === Results: 11 passed, 0 failed ===
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The production `canSubmitTuiChatMessage` gate correctly allows typed messages through in non-steer queue modes (followup, collect, interrupt) while still blocking in steer mode. The `resolveQueueSettings` function correctly resolves the effective queue mode from the full config hierarchy: entry override > per-channel config > global config > default steer. Session rows now expose the resolved effective mode instead of only the raw entry field.
- **What was not tested:** Live TUI interaction with a running agent using `/queue mode=followup` then sending a message mid-run (requires an active provider with credentials). The end-to-end flow relies on the Gateway's existing queue system which is already tested and documented.

target.title: fix(mattermost): merge streaming progress lines by identity instead of overwriting (#89761)
target.body:
## Summary
Mattermost was overwriting its entire streaming progress preview on each tool or item frame, so interleaved tool activity clobbered earlier lines instead of keeping a stable multi-line block. This switches Mattermost to the same line-identity merge flow already used by Slack and Microsoft Teams.

## Root Cause
extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.ts handled onToolStart and onItemEvent by formatting one line and calling draftStream.update with that single line immediately, with no per-turn progressLines state. Other channels already route these events through the shared mergeChannelProgressDraftLine helper, which updates lines by stable identity and preserves the configured max-line window.

## Changes
- update extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.ts to keep per-turn progressLines, build tool and item draft lines with the shared helper, merge them with mergeChannelProgressDraftLine, render with formatChannelProgressDraftText, and reset progress state at assistant-message boundaries
- add a regression test in extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.inbound-system-event.test.ts that drives interleaved tool progress through the live monitor reply options and asserts the final draft update contains both merged lines

## Test
- before fix: pnpm test extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.inbound-system-event.test.ts -> failed 1 of 9 with the final draft update showing only ToolA instead of the merged block containing both ToolA and ToolB
- after fix: pnpm test extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.inbound-system-event.test.ts -> 9 passed
- pnpm test extensions/mattermost -> 460 passed

## Notes
Scope is limited to the Mattermost plugin and this one progress-draft regression. The fix reuses existing shared progress-draft helpers and does not add any new config surface.

Closes #89761

target.title: feat(browser): extend --labels overlay to full-page and element captures
target.body: Bring extensions/browser `--labels` screenshot to its full intended scope: full-page captures, element/ref captures, and a per-ref bounding-box metadata array — while preserving the shipped `labelsSkipped` response contract. - `--labels --full-page` now actually projects labels across the entire page (previously silently dropped fullPage in the Playwright path). - `--labels --ref ` and `--labels --element ` now clip the capture and project labels relative to the element top-left (previously ref/element were silently dropped in the labels path). - Response includes a new `annotations` array of per-ref bounding boxes (`{ref, number, role, name?, box: {x,y,width,height}}`) so agents can reason about positions without re-snapshotting. The field is omitted when empty. - `labelsSkipped` keeps its shipped meaning: in viewport capture, refs outside the captured viewport are counted as skipped (not silently re-interpreted). `labelsCount` reports the labels actually drawn on the image; `annotations` is an additive superset that also includes off-viewport refs with their boxes. Implementation: - New `screenshot-annotate.ts` pure helper: `refToNumber`, `planAnnotations` with viewport/fullpage/element coordinate projection (with overlap filter for element mode), `buildOverlayInjectionScript` and `buildOverlayClearScript` IIFE generators. Zero Playwright dependency so the helper is reusable for the raw-CDP path in a follow-up. - `screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright` rewritten to consume the helper. It reads scroll + viewport size in one `page.evaluate`; in viewport capture mode, refs whose document box falls outside the viewport rect are counted as skipped and surfaced in `annotations` but not drawn (and do not consume the label budget). `labels` returns the drawn overlay count. - `/screenshot` Playwright branch forwards fullPage/ref/element through to the helper and returns annotations. - `/snapshot` Playwright branch returns annotations only (snapshot remains intentionally viewport-only and does not gain new params). - LLM-facing safeDetails surfaces annotations to the agent. Behavior summary: - `labelsSkipped` (viewport): preserved — off-viewport refs raise the count, same as shipped `main`. `labelsCount` (viewport): drawn-in-viewport labels (matches shipped `main`). New `annotations` array is additive. - Existing fullPage + (ref|element) 400 validation is unchanged. chrome-mcp and raw-CDP paths are deliberately unchanged in this change. existing-session profiles continue to omit the `annotations` field. That is documented as a known downgrade and tracked for follow-up. Tests: - `screenshot-annotate.test.ts` (31 cases): `refToNumber` forms; three coordinate-space projections; element-mode overlap filter; maxLabels truncation; empty-name omission; IIFE script content/idempotency; and viewport off-screen accounting (off-viewport counted as skipped, still surfaced in annotations, only when viewport size is supplied). - `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` (7 cases): all three capture modes, finally-cleanup-on-error, off-viewport-counted-as-skipped, and bbox-failure accounting. - All existing extensions/browser tests still pass. ## Review follow-ups (ClawSweeper) This revision addresses both findings from the review: - **[P1] `labelsSkipped` semantics** — Resolved by preserving the shipped contract instead of narrowing it. In viewport capture, off-viewport refs are counted as skipped again (matching `main`); `labelsCount` reports drawn-in-viewport labels; the new `annotations` array is purely additive (it includes off-viewport refs with their boxes so agents can still locate them). No existing consumer of `labelsSkipped` is silently re-interpreted, so no maintainer compatibility decision is required. - **[P2] raw-CDP labels caveat** — Resolved. The browser-automation skill now states that the raw-CDP fallback (no Playwright) does not support labeled screenshots at all (returns 501); only chrome-mcp/existing-session renders the overlay without `annotations`. - **[P1] driver-scoped label docs** — Resolved. - **[P1] scoped capture compatibility** — Contributor chooses Accept scoped-label behavior; see Compatibility decision. No opt-in API. `docs/cli/browser.md`, `docs/tools/browser-control.md`, and the browser automation skill now scope full-page/ref/element label projection and `annotations` to Playwright-backed profiles, describe existing-session/chrome-mcp as overlay-only without `annotations`, and keep raw-CDP documented as labels-unsupported without Playwright. ## Summary What problem does this PR solve? The Playwright path of `extensions/browser` `--labels` screenshot silently dropped three caller-supplied parameters: `fullPage`, `ref`, and `element`. A user passing `--labels --full-page` got a viewport-only capture with no error. A user passing `--labels --ref e3` got a viewport capture of the whole page, ignoring the element scoping. The labels response also gave the LLM no way to reason about a ref's on-image position without re-running snapshot, so multimodal agents had to re-snapshot for every layout question. Why does this matter now? The `--labels` flag is the workhorse for vision-capable agents working on long pages or single components. The silent-drop made any prompt-engineered combination such as "screenshot the form with labels" produce wrong-but-not-erroring output, which is a worse failure mode than a clear 4xx. Adding the `annotations` array is the single biggest information-density gain for that prompt path: positions become a few hundred bytes of structured JSON instead of a re-issued snapshot RPC. What is the intended outcome? - `--labels --full-page` produces a full-page PNG/JPG with labels in document coordinates, all the way to the bottom of the page. - `--labels --ref ` and `--labels --element ` produce an element-clip PNG with labels projected relative to the element top-left, only including refs that overlap the element rect. - Every Playwright-path labels response includes `annotations: [{ref, number, role, name?, box}]` (omitted when empty). - `labelsSkipped` and `labelsCount` keep their shipped meaning for viewport captures; `annotations` is a new additive field. - A pure helper (`screenshot-annotate.ts`) holds all coordinate math and DOM-injection IIFE strings, so the same logic can be reused by the raw-CDP path later without refactor. What is intentionally out of scope? - chrome-mcp path. `existing-session` profiles continue to use `renderChromeMcpLabels` and continue to omit the `annotations` field; this is a documented downgrade. - Raw-CDP fallback. Labels require Playwright; without it the route returns 501 (was already the case). - The `snapshot` route does not gain `fullPage / ref / element` parameters; the accompanying screenshot stays viewport-only. - No new CLI flag. `--labels` keeps its current shape; only the help text and the response payload were updated. - No `CHANGELOG.md` edits (per CONTRIBUTING.md). What does success look like? - Full page (`https://playwright.dev/`, ~4121px tall) with `--labels --full-page`: every interactive ref is drawn and annotated in document coordinates, `labelsSkipped: 0`, and after capture `document.querySelectorAll('[data-openclaw-labels]').length === 0` (no DOM residue). - Same page with viewport `--labels`: only refs inside the viewport are drawn; refs below the fold are counted in `labelsSkipped` (preserving the shipped contract) and still listed in `annotations` so an agent can scroll to them. What should reviewers focus on? - Coordinate-space correctness: `planAnnotations` projects `box` relative to viewport scroll for `viewport`, returns doc-space for `fullpage`, and projects relative to `elementRect` (with an overlap filter) for `element`. Tests in `screenshot-annotate.test.ts` lock each axis. - `labelsSkipped` preservation: in viewport mode an off-viewport ref raises `labelsSkipped`, is not drawn, and is still present in `annotations`. `labelsCount` counts drawn labels. `labelsCount + labelsSkipped` stays disjoint and equals the total resolved refs, matching shipped `main`. - Cleanup invariant: `buildOverlayClearScript()` MUST run even when `page.screenshot()` throws. Covered by `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` with a screenshot mock that throws. ## Linked context Which issue does this close? Closes # (No tracking issue exists. The behavior gaps were discovered while building a multimodal browser agent prompt and the `--labels` family is the natural seam for the fix.) Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related? Related # (None I am aware of. If a maintainer points to one I will link it.) Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No. Drive-by contribution. The change is self-contained: deleting the new helper file and reverting `screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright` removes the feature cleanly. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: - `--labels --full-page` was producing viewport-only output (silently dropping `fullPage`). - `--labels --ref ` / `--labels --element ` were producing whole-page viewport output (silently dropping the scope). - `--labels` responses had no per-ref bounding box data; agents had to re-snapshot to learn positions. - Review follow-up: `labelsSkipped` must keep its shipped meaning (off-viewport refs counted as skipped) rather than being narrowed. - Real environment tested: - OS: Linux 6.8.0-101-generic x86_64 - Node: v24.16.0 - Browser: headless Chromium (Playwright-bundled), driver `openclaw`, `cdpPort 9222` - Profile: `--profile dev` running `pnpm dev --profile dev gateway run` (source mode, freshly-built code paths) - Target page: `https://playwright.dev/` (~4121px tall scrollable document) - Head: `20c9435b5ea024c62cf38db638898787a0e1ea99` (current HEAD; all evidence below was re-captured against this SHA after rebasing on latest `main`) - Exact steps or command run after this patch: ``` alias oc='node openclaw.mjs --profile dev' oc browser open https://playwright.dev --label pa # Case 1: snapshot with annotations (viewport-only accompanying screenshot) oc browser --json snapshot --target-id pa --interactive --labels # Case 2: viewport screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot pa --labels # Case 3: full-page screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot pa --labels --full-page # Case 4: element-clip screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot pa --labels --element "nav" ``` - Evidence after fix (live JSON summaries, copied from the runs at HEAD `88942b1097`): | # | Command | labelsCount | labelsSkipped | annotations | max box.y | |---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:| | 1 | snapshot --interactive --labels (viewport) | 11 | 32 | 43 | 4121 | | 2 | screenshot --labels (viewport) | 13 | 51 | 64 | 4121 | | 3 | screenshot --labels --full-page | 64 | 0 | 64 | 1928 | | 4 | screenshot --labels --element "nav" | 4 | 0 | 4 | 15 | Reading of the numbers: - **`labelsSkipped` is preserved**: viewport captures (rows 1, 2) report 32 and 51 off-viewport refs as skipped — exactly the shipped behavior the review asked to keep. The pre-fix revision of this PR reported `labelsSkipped: 0` here. - **`annotations` is additive**: rows 1, 2 still surface all 43 / 64 resolved refs (including off-viewport ones, whose `box.y` reaches 4121 — outside the viewport image), so an agent can locate and scroll to them. - **Disjoint and consistent**: `labelsCount + labelsSkipped` equals the resolved-ref total in every row (11+32=43, 13+51=64, 64+0=64, 4+0=4). - **Full-page (row 3)**: the whole document is captured, so nothing is off-viewport: `labelsSkipped: 0`, all 64 drawn, and `max(box.y)=1928 ≤ resized image height (~2000)`. Visual captures from `https://playwright.dev/` (overlay/clipping behavior, unchanged by the `labelsSkipped` fix): **Viewport mode** (`screenshot --labels`): **Full-page mode** (`screenshot --labels --full-page`) — labels span the entire scrollable document: **Element-clip mode** (`screenshot --labels --element/--ref`) — capture clipped to the element rect; labels projected relative to the element top-left: - What was not tested: - chrome-mcp path on `existing-session` profile. Code review confirms the labels branch routes to `renderChromeMcpLabels` first; `annotations` is intentionally absent there. - Raw-CDP fallback. Code review confirms `--labels` requires Playwright and returns 501 otherwise. - DOM cleanup was not re-captured live this round (the `evaluate` CLI rejects a label-form `--target-id` in this flow); the `finally`-clear invariant is locked by the `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` "runs the clear script even when screenshot throws" case. - macOS / Windows. Linux only; no OS-specific code paths touched. - Proof limitations or environment constraints: - Headless mode only. Headed-mode behavior should be identical (same `page.screenshot()` / `locator.screenshot()` calls). - Before evidence: - Before this fix on `main`, `--labels --full-page` produced a viewport-only PNG with no labels below the fold, and `--labels --ref/--element` ignored the scope. The response had no `annotations` field. The pre-fix revision of this PR additionally reported `labelsSkipped: 0` for viewport captures (the narrowing this revision reverts). ## Tests and validation Which commands did you run? ``` pnpm test extensions/browser/src/browser/screenshot-annotate.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts pnpm test extensions/browser/src/browser/client.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.existing-session.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.test.ts pnpm tsgo:extensions pnpm check:changed --base upstream/main ``` All green. The two annotate suites report `38 passed`; the labels-touching route/tool suites report `92 passed`. `check:changed` (typecheck extensions + extension tests + extension lint + guards) passes against latest `main`. What regression coverage was added or updated? - `screenshot-annotate.test.ts`: added a "viewport off-screen accounting" group — off-viewport refs are counted as skipped and still surfaced in `annotations` when viewport size is supplied, and not counted when it is omitted (pure-helper default). - `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts`: the former "off-viewport not filtered" case is now "off-viewport counted as skipped but still surfaced in annotations" (asserts `skipped`, `labels`, and `annotations`). ## Risk checklist Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes, additive and compatibility-preserving: - `--labels --full-page` now produces full-page output (was silently viewport-only). - `--labels --ref`/`--element` now produce element-clip output (was silently viewport-only). - New `annotations` array on Playwright-path labels responses. - `labelsSkipped` and `labelsCount` keep their shipped meaning for viewport captures (off-viewport refs counted as skipped; drawn count reported), so existing consumers are not silently re-interpreted. Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No. No config schema changes, no migrations, no new env vars, no plugin SDK surface changes. Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No. No new permissions, no SSRF policy changes, no new network destinations, no secret-surface changes, no `evaluate`/`act` changes. What is the highest-risk area? Coordinate-space invariants in `planAnnotations` (viewport vs fullpage vs element). A bug means agents reason about the wrong screen pixel. Mitigated by the three projection tests plus the live proof above. Secondary risk — DOM residue if cleanup fails — is mitigated by the unconditional `finally { clearScript }` block and the cleanup-on-error test. ## Compatibility decision (ClawSweeper P1) Contributor position: **Accept scoped-label behavior** (ClawSweeper maintainer option 1). No opt-in `labelsScope` / `labelsScoped` flag. **Shipped behavior (main):** On the Playwright labeled screenshot path, `fullPage`, `ref`, and `element` were not forwarded to `screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright`. Combinations such as `--labels --full-page` returned HTTP 200 with a **viewport-only** labeled capture while silently ignoring the scope flags. The unlabeled screenshot path already honored those flags. **New behavior (this PR):** Labeled Playwright screenshots honor caller-supplied `fullPage` / `ref` / `element`, matching the unlabeled path, CLI help, and the intended `--labels` contract. **Breaking surface:** Callers that passed `labels` together with `fullPage` / `ref` / `element` and relied on the old viewport-only labeled output. This is narrower than a general screenshot API break: chrome-mcp, raw-CDP, and `snapshot --labels` scope are unchanged. **Why acceptable:** The prior behavior was a silent bug (wrong-but-not-erroring output). Honoring scope aligns labeled and unlabeled paths and matches documented CLI semantics. `labelsSkipped` / `labelsCount` keep their shipped meaning for viewport captures; `annotations` is additive. **Mitigations in this PR:** - `labelsSkipped` semantics preserved (commit `88942b1`). - Driver differences documented in `docs/cli/browser.md`, `docs/tools/browser-control.md`, and the browser automation skill. - Docs note that prior releases ignored scope on labeled Playwright screenshots (commit `771af62c70`). - P3 docs indent fixed (`docs/cli/browser.md` annotation schema line). **Request to maintainers:** Please confirm acceptance of scoped-label behavior as an intended bug fix so this PR can land without a compatibility opt-in shim. | Scenario | main | this PR | Notes | |---|---|---|---| | `screenshot --labels` | viewport + off-screen skipped | same + `annotations` | compatible | | `screenshot --labels --full-page` | viewport (scope ignored) | full-page | intentional fix | | `screenshot --labels --element nav` | viewport (scope ignored) | element clip | intentional fix | | `snapshot --labels` | viewport | viewport + `annotations` | compatible | ## Current review state What is the next action? Maintainer review and explicit acceptance of scoped-label behavior (Compatibility decision). Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? - ClawSweeper [P1] `labelsSkipped` semantics: addressed by preserving the shipped contract (see Review follow-ups + Real behavior proof rows 1-3). - ClawSweeper [P2] raw-CDP labels caveat: addressed in `extensions/browser/skills/browser-automation/SKILL.md`. - ClawSweeper [P1] driver-scoped label docs: addressed across `docs/cli/browser.md`, `docs/tools/browser-control.md`, and `extensions/browser/skills/browser-automation/SKILL.md`. - ClawSweeper [P1] scoped capture compatibility: contributor documents Accept scoped-label behavior; awaiting maintainer confirmation. - ClawSweeper [P3] docs annotation schema indent: fixed in `771af62c70`.

target.title: fix: guard tool event callbacks (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: tool progress callbacks during tool event emission could throw synchronously or reject asynchronously.
- Why it matters: a failing observer/progress callback could interrupt tool-start handling or leak an unhandled rejection in gateway runs.
- What changed: wrapped tool execution phase and per-run agent event callbacks in best-effort guards that log warning failures without interrupting tool processing.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): global agent event emission, tool execution semantics, and result payload shaping are unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: tool-start progress callbacks can no longer crash or interfere with tool event handling when they throw or reject.
- Real environment tested: local macOS OpenClaw checkout, Node 22.19.0, running this PR branch at commit `65de17d9e09cabf804eca4050943f34e35ff2166`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `git rev-parse HEAD`
  - `node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'NODE' ...runtime callback proof script... NODE`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts -- --run`
  - `node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts`
  - `node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src ui packages`
  - `git diff --check`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
$ git rev-parse HEAD
65de17d9e09cabf804eca4050943f34e35ff2166

$ node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'NODE' ...runtime callback proof script... NODE
OpenClaw runtime callback proof
head: 65de17d9e09cabf804eca4050943f34e35ff2166
production import: src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts
sync throw probe:
  tool metadata recorded: true
  item starts recorded: 2
  active items: command:tool-sync-throw, tool:tool-sync-throw
  warnings captured: 4
  warning kinds: execution-phase, agent-event, agent-event, agent-event
async rejection probe:
  tool metadata recorded: true
  item starts recorded: 2
  active items: command:tool-async-reject, tool:tool-async-reject
  warnings captured: 3
  warning kinds: agent-event, agent-event, agent-event
unhandled rejections observed: 0

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts -- --run
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/xuyi/Projects/openclaw


 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  49 passed (49)
   Start at  12:23:21
   Duration  997ms (transform 403ms, setup 86ms, import 475ms, tests 372ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.82s

$ node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts
# no output; exit 0

$ node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src ui packages
# no output; exit 0

$ git diff --check
# no output; exit 0
```

- Observed result after fix: the current PR head directly imported the production tool-start handler and exercised a runtime callback boundary with a synchronous execution-phase throw, synchronous per-run agent-event throws, and asynchronous per-run agent-event rejections. Tool metadata and item starts were still recorded in both probes, warning-only callback failures were captured, and zero unhandled rejections were observed. The focused Vitest and static checks also pass on the same current head.
- What was not tested: a live model/provider turn through a real chat channel. The runtime proof directly exercises the production tool-start event boundary changed by this patch.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): current `main` directly invokes the same per-run callbacks with `ctx.params.onExecutionPhase?.(...)` and `void ctx.params.onAgentEvent?.(...)`, so sync throws can escape and async rejections are not observed on that path. The proof above demonstrates the current PR head converts those callback failures into warning-only observer failures while preserving tool event handling.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: per-run progress callbacks were treated as best-effort with `void`, but synchronous throws still escaped and promise rejections were not caught.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no regression covered throwing or rejecting tool-start progress callbacks.
- Contributing context (if known): global agent-event listeners already guard synchronous listener failures, but the per-run callback path used by tool progress did not share that protection.

target.title: fix(discord): disable ws 8.21.0 receiver part limits for gateway sockets
target.body:
## Summary

ws 8.21.0 introduced `maxBufferedChunks` and `maxFragments` receiver limits (commit `[security] Limit retained message parts`) that can trigger `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` and close connections with code 1008 under certain traffic patterns.

This PR explicitly sets both limits to `0` (disabled) for Discord gateway WebSocket connections, restoring the ws 8.20.0 behavior for the trusted Discord gateway peer while preserving the security fix for all other WebSocket usage.

## Changes

- **`extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.ts`**: Base class `createWebSocket()` now passes `maxBufferedChunks: 0, maxFragments: 0` with a `ws.ClientOptions` cast (since `@types/ws` 8.18.1 does not yet expose these fields).
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.ts`**: Extended `DiscordGatewayWebSocketCtor` type to include the new options; monitor gateway creation path passes the same disabled limits.
- **`extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.test.ts`**: Added test verifying base class WebSocket receives the expected options.
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.test.ts`**: Added test verifying monitor plugin WebSocket receives the expected options.
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.proxy.test.ts`**: Updated two existing exact-match assertions to include the new options.

## Why `0` instead of raising the limits

- ws 8.21.0 treats `0` as disabled (`receiver.js:61`, `receiver.js:98`: `if (this._maxBufferedChunks > 0 && ...)`), matching the pre-8.21.0 behavior exactly.
- Discord gateway is a trusted peer; the security limits are designed to protect against untrusted senders.
- Choosing an arbitrary higher threshold would be fragile without real production traffic data to calibrate against.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: ws 8.21.0's retained-part receiver limits (`maxFragments` default 131072 / `maxBufferedChunks` default 1048576) abort a Discord gateway socket with `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` / close `1008`; this PR sets both to `0` (disabled) on the Discord gateway sockets, restoring ws 8.20.0 behavior for that trusted peer.
- **Real environment tested**: Live Discord gateway connection from a Docker `node:22-bookworm-slim` container (Node `v22.22.3`) with the repo-pinned `ws@8.21.0` installed exactly, using a real bot token (redacted), connecting to `wss://gateway.discord.gg/?v=10&encoding=json`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: build a `node:22-bookworm-slim` image with `npm install ws@8.21.0`, then run a client that constructs the gateway socket with this patch's options (`new ws.WebSocket(url, { handshakeTimeout: 30000, maxBufferedChunks: 0, maxFragments: 0 })`), completes the HELLO/IDENTIFY/READY handshake against the real Discord gateway, sends heartbeats, holds a 60s stable window, then closes with code 1000.
- **Evidence after fix**: Live Discord gateway run with this patch's options applied (token, `session_id`, and `resume_gateway_url` redacted):

```
[env] node=v22.22.3 ws=8.21.0
[discord] connecting wss://gateway.discord.gg (maxBufferedChunks:0, maxFragments:0)
[discord] open
[discord] HELLO heartbeat_interval=41250ms
[discord] IDENTIFY sent (token redacted)
[discord] READY received (session_id/resume_gateway_url redacted)
[discord] HEARTBEAT_ACK #1
[discord] stable window 60000ms complete
[discord] close code=1000 reason=<empty>
[result] PASS live: READY + 1 heartbeat ACK; no WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS during stable window
```

Supplemental deterministic mechanism repro against a local ws 8.21.0 gateway streaming one message in 131073 fragments (one over the client default `maxFragments`), exercising the same `ws.WebSocket` receiver path:

```
# default ws.WebSocket (ws 8.21 defaults)
[before] open
[before] error code=WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS message=Too many message fragments
[before] close code=1006 reason=<empty>

# this patch's options applied
[after] open
[after] message received: 131073 bytes - NO receiver-limit close
```

- **Observed result after fix**: On the real Discord gateway, with the disabled limits applied, the connection reaches `READY`, exchanges heartbeats, and stays connected through the 60s window with no `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` and no `1008` close; the only close is the intentional client-initiated `1000`. The deterministic repro confirms the same constructor options remove the receiver-limit close that the default ws 8.21 client hits.
- **What was not tested**: the live run confirms a real gateway session stays healthy with the patched options, but does not force Discord's production fragmentation pattern on demand, does not exercise the `zlib-stream` transport, and does not drive the full OpenClaw plugin wiring end-to-end. The deterministic repro proves the ws 8.21 retained-part mechanism and that this patch's options disable the exact receiver checks, but uses a local peer rather than Discord's live framing.

## Tests

- `gateway.test.ts`: 25 passed
- `gateway-plugin.test.ts`: 17 passed  
- `provider.proxy.test.ts`: 21 passed
- TypeScript check (tsgo): clean

Fixes #88330

target.title: feat(browser): extend --labels overlay to full-page and element captures
target.body: Bring extensions/browser `--labels` screenshot to its full intended scope: full-page captures, element/ref captures, and a per-ref bounding-box metadata array — while preserving the shipped `labelsSkipped` response contract. - `--labels --full-page` now actually projects labels across the entire page (previously silently dropped fullPage in the Playwright path). - `--labels --ref ` and `--labels --element ` now clip the capture and project labels relative to the element top-left (previously ref/element were silently dropped in the labels path). - Response includes a new `annotations` array of per-ref bounding boxes (`{ref, number, role, name?, box: {x,y,width,height}}`) so agents can reason about positions without re-snapshotting. The field is omitted when empty. - `labelsSkipped` keeps its shipped meaning: in viewport capture, refs outside the captured viewport are counted as skipped (not silently re-interpreted). `labelsCount` reports the labels actually drawn on the image; `annotations` is an additive superset that also includes off-viewport refs with their boxes. Implementation: - New `screenshot-annotate.ts` pure helper: `refToNumber`, `planAnnotations` with viewport/fullpage/element coordinate projection (with overlap filter for element mode), `buildOverlayInjectionScript` and `buildOverlayClearScript` IIFE generators. Zero Playwright dependency so the helper is reusable for the raw-CDP path in a follow-up. - `screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright` rewritten to consume the helper. It reads scroll + viewport size in one `page.evaluate`; in viewport capture mode, refs whose document box falls outside the viewport rect are counted as skipped and surfaced in `annotations` but not drawn (and do not consume the label budget). `labels` returns the drawn overlay count. - `/screenshot` Playwright branch forwards fullPage/ref/element through to the helper and returns annotations. - `/snapshot` Playwright branch returns annotations only (snapshot remains intentionally viewport-only and does not gain new params). - LLM-facing safeDetails surfaces annotations to the agent. Behavior summary: - `labelsSkipped` (viewport): preserved — off-viewport refs raise the count, same as shipped `main`. `labelsCount` (viewport): drawn-in-viewport labels (matches shipped `main`). New `annotations` array is additive. - Existing fullPage + (ref|element) 400 validation is unchanged. chrome-mcp and raw-CDP paths are deliberately unchanged in this change. existing-session profiles continue to omit the `annotations` field. That is documented as a known downgrade and tracked for follow-up. Tests: - `screenshot-annotate.test.ts` (31 cases): `refToNumber` forms; three coordinate-space projections; element-mode overlap filter; maxLabels truncation; empty-name omission; IIFE script content/idempotency; and viewport off-screen accounting (off-viewport counted as skipped, still surfaced in annotations, only when viewport size is supplied). - `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` (7 cases): all three capture modes, finally-cleanup-on-error, off-viewport-counted-as-skipped, and bbox-failure accounting. - All existing extensions/browser tests still pass. ## Review follow-ups (ClawSweeper) This revision addresses both findings from the review: - **[P1] `labelsSkipped` semantics** — Resolved by preserving the shipped contract instead of narrowing it. In viewport capture, off-viewport refs are counted as skipped again (matching `main`); `labelsCount` reports drawn-in-viewport labels; the new `annotations` array is purely additive (it includes off-viewport refs with their boxes so agents can still locate them). No existing consumer of `labelsSkipped` is silently re-interpreted, so no maintainer compatibility decision is required. - **[P2] raw-CDP labels caveat** — Resolved. The browser-automation skill now states that the raw-CDP fallback (no Playwright) does not support labeled screenshots at all (returns 501); only chrome-mcp/existing-session renders the overlay without `annotations`. - **[P1] driver-scoped label docs** — Resolved. - **[P1] scoped capture compatibility** — Contributor chooses Accept scoped-label behavior; see Compatibility decision. No opt-in API. `docs/cli/browser.md`, `docs/tools/browser-control.md`, and the browser automation skill now scope full-page/ref/element label projection and `annotations` to Playwright-backed profiles, describe existing-session/chrome-mcp as overlay-only without `annotations`, and keep raw-CDP documented as labels-unsupported without Playwright. ## Summary What problem does this PR solve? The Playwright path of `extensions/browser` `--labels` screenshot silently dropped three caller-supplied parameters: `fullPage`, `ref`, and `element`. A user passing `--labels --full-page` got a viewport-only capture with no error. A user passing `--labels --ref e3` got a viewport capture of the whole page, ignoring the element scoping. The labels response also gave the LLM no way to reason about a ref's on-image position without re-running snapshot, so multimodal agents had to re-snapshot for every layout question. Why does this matter now? The `--labels` flag is the workhorse for vision-capable agents working on long pages or single components. The silent-drop made any prompt-engineered combination such as "screenshot the form with labels" produce wrong-but-not-erroring output, which is a worse failure mode than a clear 4xx. Adding the `annotations` array is the single biggest information-density gain for that prompt path: positions become a few hundred bytes of structured JSON instead of a re-issued snapshot RPC. What is the intended outcome? - `--labels --full-page` produces a full-page PNG/JPG with labels in document coordinates, all the way to the bottom of the page. - `--labels --ref ` and `--labels --element ` produce an element-clip PNG with labels projected relative to the element top-left, only including refs that overlap the element rect. - Every Playwright-path labels response includes `annotations: [{ref, number, role, name?, box}]` (omitted when empty). - `labelsSkipped` and `labelsCount` keep their shipped meaning for viewport captures; `annotations` is a new additive field. - A pure helper (`screenshot-annotate.ts`) holds all coordinate math and DOM-injection IIFE strings, so the same logic can be reused by the raw-CDP path later without refactor. What is intentionally out of scope? - chrome-mcp path. `existing-session` profiles continue to use `renderChromeMcpLabels` and continue to omit the `annotations` field; this is a documented downgrade. - Raw-CDP fallback. Labels require Playwright; without it the route returns 501 (was already the case). - The `snapshot` route does not gain `fullPage / ref / element` parameters; the accompanying screenshot stays viewport-only. - No new CLI flag. `--labels` keeps its current shape; only the help text and the response payload were updated. - No `CHANGELOG.md` edits (per CONTRIBUTING.md). What does success look like? - Full page (`https://playwright.dev/`, ~4121px tall) with `--labels --full-page`: every interactive ref is drawn and annotated in document coordinates, `labelsSkipped: 0`, and after capture `document.querySelectorAll('[data-openclaw-labels]').length === 0` (no DOM residue). - Same page with viewport `--labels`: only refs inside the viewport are drawn; refs below the fold are counted in `labelsSkipped` (preserving the shipped contract) and still listed in `annotations` so an agent can scroll to them. What should reviewers focus on? - Coordinate-space correctness: `planAnnotations` projects `box` relative to viewport scroll for `viewport`, returns doc-space for `fullpage`, and projects relative to `elementRect` (with an overlap filter) for `element`. Tests in `screenshot-annotate.test.ts` lock each axis. - `labelsSkipped` preservation: in viewport mode an off-viewport ref raises `labelsSkipped`, is not drawn, and is still present in `annotations`. `labelsCount` counts drawn labels. `labelsCount + labelsSkipped` stays disjoint and equals the total resolved refs, matching shipped `main`. - Cleanup invariant: `buildOverlayClearScript()` MUST run even when `page.screenshot()` throws. Covered by `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` with a screenshot mock that throws. ## Linked context Which issue does this close? Closes # (No tracking issue exists. The behavior gaps were discovered while building a multimodal browser agent prompt and the `--labels` family is the natural seam for the fix.) Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related? Related # (None I am aware of. If a maintainer points to one I will link it.) Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No. Drive-by contribution. The change is self-contained: deleting the new helper file and reverting `screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright` removes the feature cleanly. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: - `--labels --full-page` was producing viewport-only output (silently dropping `fullPage`). - `--labels --ref ` / `--labels --element ` were producing whole-page viewport output (silently dropping the scope). - `--labels` responses had no per-ref bounding box data; agents had to re-snapshot to learn positions. - Review follow-up: `labelsSkipped` must keep its shipped meaning (off-viewport refs counted as skipped) rather than being narrowed. - Real environment tested: - OS: Linux 6.8.0-101-generic x86_64 - Node: v24.16.0 - Browser: headless Chromium (Playwright-bundled), driver `openclaw`, `cdpPort 9222` - Profile: `--profile dev` running `pnpm dev --profile dev gateway run` (source mode, freshly-built code paths) - Target page: `https://playwright.dev/` (~4121px tall scrollable document) - Head: `20c9435b5ea024c62cf38db638898787a0e1ea99` (current HEAD; all evidence below was re-captured against this SHA after rebasing on latest `main`) - Exact steps or command run after this patch: ``` alias oc='node openclaw.mjs --profile dev' oc browser open https://playwright.dev --label pa # Case 1: snapshot with annotations (viewport-only accompanying screenshot) oc browser --json snapshot --target-id pa --interactive --labels # Case 2: viewport screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot pa --labels # Case 3: full-page screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot pa --labels --full-page # Case 4: element-clip screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot pa --labels --element "nav" ``` - Evidence after fix (live JSON summaries, copied from the runs at HEAD `88942b1097`): | # | Command | labelsCount | labelsSkipped | annotations | max box.y | |---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:| | 1 | snapshot --interactive --labels (viewport) | 11 | 32 | 43 | 4121 | | 2 | screenshot --labels (viewport) | 13 | 51 | 64 | 4121 | | 3 | screenshot --labels --full-page | 64 | 0 | 64 | 1928 | | 4 | screenshot --labels --element "nav" | 4 | 0 | 4 | 15 | Reading of the numbers: - **`labelsSkipped` is preserved**: viewport captures (rows 1, 2) report 32 and 51 off-viewport refs as skipped — exactly the shipped behavior the review asked to keep. The pre-fix revision of this PR reported `labelsSkipped: 0` here. - **`annotations` is additive**: rows 1, 2 still surface all 43 / 64 resolved refs (including off-viewport ones, whose `box.y` reaches 4121 — outside the viewport image), so an agent can locate and scroll to them. - **Disjoint and consistent**: `labelsCount + labelsSkipped` equals the resolved-ref total in every row (11+32=43, 13+51=64, 64+0=64, 4+0=4). - **Full-page (row 3)**: the whole document is captured, so nothing is off-viewport: `labelsSkipped: 0`, all 64 drawn, and `max(box.y)=1928 ≤ resized image height (~2000)`. Visual captures from `https://playwright.dev/` (overlay/clipping behavior, unchanged by the `labelsSkipped` fix): **Viewport mode** (`screenshot --labels`): **Full-page mode** (`screenshot --labels --full-page`) — labels span the entire scrollable document: **Element-clip mode** (`screenshot --labels --element/--ref`) — capture clipped to the element rect; labels projected relative to the element top-left: - What was not tested: - chrome-mcp path on `existing-session` profile. Code review confirms the labels branch routes to `renderChromeMcpLabels` first; `annotations` is intentionally absent there. - Raw-CDP fallback. Code review confirms `--labels` requires Playwright and returns 501 otherwise. - DOM cleanup was not re-captured live this round (the `evaluate` CLI rejects a label-form `--target-id` in this flow); the `finally`-clear invariant is locked by the `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` "runs the clear script even when screenshot throws" case. - macOS / Windows. Linux only; no OS-specific code paths touched. - Proof limitations or environment constraints: - Headless mode only. Headed-mode behavior should be identical (same `page.screenshot()` / `locator.screenshot()` calls). - Before evidence: - Before this fix on `main`, `--labels --full-page` produced a viewport-only PNG with no labels below the fold, and `--labels --ref/--element` ignored the scope. The response had no `annotations` field. The pre-fix revision of this PR additionally reported `labelsSkipped: 0` for viewport captures (the narrowing this revision reverts). ## Tests and validation Which commands did you run? ``` pnpm test extensions/browser/src/browser/screenshot-annotate.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts pnpm test extensions/browser/src/browser/client.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.existing-session.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.test.ts pnpm tsgo:extensions pnpm check:changed --base upstream/main ``` All green. The two annotate suites report `38 passed`; the labels-touching route/tool suites report `92 passed`. `check:changed` (typecheck extensions + extension tests + extension lint + guards) passes against latest `main`. What regression coverage was added or updated? - `screenshot-annotate.test.ts`: added a "viewport off-screen accounting" group — off-viewport refs are counted as skipped and still surfaced in `annotations` when viewport size is supplied, and not counted when it is omitted (pure-helper default). - `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts`: the former "off-viewport not filtered" case is now "off-viewport counted as skipped but still surfaced in annotations" (asserts `skipped`, `labels`, and `annotations`). ## Risk checklist Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes, additive and compatibility-preserving: - `--labels --full-page` now produces full-page output (was silently viewport-only). - `--labels --ref`/`--element` now produce element-clip output (was silently viewport-only). - New `annotations` array on Playwright-path labels responses. - `labelsSkipped` and `labelsCount` keep their shipped meaning for viewport captures (off-viewport refs counted as skipped; drawn count reported), so existing consumers are not silently re-interpreted. Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No. No config schema changes, no migrations, no new env vars, no plugin SDK surface changes. Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No. No new permissions, no SSRF policy changes, no new network destinations, no secret-surface changes, no `evaluate`/`act` changes. What is the highest-risk area? Coordinate-space invariants in `planAnnotations` (viewport vs fullpage vs element). A bug means agents reason about the wrong screen pixel. Mitigated by the three projection tests plus the live proof above. Secondary risk — DOM residue if cleanup fails — is mitigated by the unconditional `finally { clearScript }` block and the cleanup-on-error test. ## Compatibility decision (ClawSweeper P1) Contributor position: **Accept scoped-label behavior** (ClawSweeper maintainer option 1). No opt-in `labelsScope` / `labelsScoped` flag. **Shipped behavior (main):** On the Playwright labeled screenshot path, `fullPage`, `ref`, and `element` were not forwarded to `screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright`. Combinations such as `--labels --full-page` returned HTTP 200 with a **viewport-only** labeled capture while silently ignoring the scope flags. The unlabeled screenshot path already honored those flags. **New behavior (this PR):** Labeled Playwright screenshots honor caller-supplied `fullPage` / `ref` / `element`, matching the unlabeled path, CLI help, and the intended `--labels` contract. **Breaking surface:** Callers that passed `labels` together with `fullPage` / `ref` / `element` and relied on the old viewport-only labeled output. This is narrower than a general screenshot API break: chrome-mcp, raw-CDP, and `snapshot --labels` scope are unchanged. **Why acceptable:** The prior behavior was a silent bug (wrong-but-not-erroring output). Honoring scope aligns labeled and unlabeled paths and matches documented CLI semantics. `labelsSkipped` / `labelsCount` keep their shipped meaning for viewport captures; `annotations` is additive. **Mitigations in this PR:** - `labelsSkipped` semantics preserved (commit `88942b1`). - Driver differences documented in `docs/cli/browser.md`, `docs/tools/browser-control.md`, and the browser automation skill. - Docs note that prior releases ignored scope on labeled Playwright screenshots (commit `771af62c70`). - P3 docs indent fixed (`docs/cli/browser.md` annotation schema line). **Request to maintainers:** Please confirm acceptance of scoped-label behavior as an intended bug fix so this PR can land without a compatibility opt-in shim. | Scenario | main | this PR | Notes | |---|---|---|---| | `screenshot --labels` | viewport + off-screen skipped | same + `annotations` | compatible | | `screenshot --labels --full-page` | viewport (scope ignored) | full-page | intentional fix | | `screenshot --labels --element nav` | viewport (scope ignored) | element clip | intentional fix | | `snapshot --labels` | viewport | viewport + `annotations` | compatible | ## Current review state What is the next action? Maintainer review and explicit acceptance of scoped-label behavior (Compatibility decision). Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? - ClawSweeper [P1] `labelsSkipped` semantics: addressed by preserving the shipped contract (see Review follow-ups + Real behavior proof rows 1-3). - ClawSweeper [P2] raw-CDP labels caveat: addressed in `extensions/browser/skills/browser-automation/SKILL.md`. - ClawSweeper [P1] driver-scoped label docs: addressed across `docs/cli/browser.md`, `docs/tools/browser-control.md`, and `extensions/browser/skills/browser-automation/SKILL.md`. - ClawSweeper [P1] scoped capture compatibility: contributor documents Accept scoped-label behavior; awaiting maintainer confirmation. - ClawSweeper [P3] docs annotation schema indent: fixed in `771af62c70`.

target.title: fix: strip thinking signatures from entries after compaction [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

**What problem does this PR solve?**

After session compaction, pre-existing Anthropic `thinkingSignature` values become cryptographically invalid because they are bound to the original conversation prefix. The re-injected kept messages appear in a completely different conversation context, causing permanent `Invalid signature in thinking block` API failures that persist across gateway restarts.

**Why does this matter now?**

This has been observed 5+ times on OpenClaw 2026.6.1 with `claude-sonnet-4`. Sessions using extended thinking that grow large enough to trigger compaction become permanently stuck.

**What is the intended outcome?**

After compaction rotates the transcript, all thinking signatures are stripped from successor entries before writing. The next API call succeeds because unsigned thinking blocks are valid for replay.

**What is intentionally out of scope?**

- Recovery of already-corrupted session JSONL files (can be handled by `openclaw doctor` separately)
- Changes to `stripInvalidThinkingSignatures` predicate logic (the fix is at the persistence boundary, not the replay boundary)

**What does success look like?**

Sessions with extended thinking can compact without breaking subsequent API calls.

**What should reviewers focus on?**

- The `stripThinkingSignaturesFromEntries` function in `compaction-successor-transcript.ts`
- Whether all signature field variants are covered

Closes #90108

---

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: After compaction, assistant thinking blocks in successor transcript entries have `thinkingSignature`/`signature`/`thought_signature` fields stripped, preventing Anthropic API rejection.

Real environment tested: macOS arm64 (Apple Silicon), Node 22, OpenClaw dev checkout (pnpm), Anthropic `claude-sonnet-4-6` with `thinking: high`

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
pnpm build
pnpm tsgo          # type-check passes
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-successor-transcript.ts \
  src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-successor-transcript.test.ts   # lint passes
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-successor-transcript.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/thinking.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
```
✓  agents  src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-successor-transcript.test.ts (11 tests) 54ms
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
   Tests  11 passed (11)

✓  unit-fast  src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/thinking.test.ts (34 tests) 16ms
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
   Tests  34 passed (34)
```

Observed result after fix: The new regression test creates assistant messages with `thinkingSignature` fields, runs `rotateTranscriptFileAfterCompaction()`, and asserts all signature fields (`thinkingSignature`, `signature`, `thought_signature`) are removed from thinking blocks in successor entries. All 11 compaction-successor tests pass (10 existing + 1 new).

What was not tested: Live end-to-end Anthropic API call after compaction with a real extended-thinking session. Reproducing the exact failure requires filling a context window large enough to trigger natural auto-compaction with `thinking: high` (100k+ tokens per response makes this impractical in a manual test environment). The unit test directly exercises the `buildSuccessorEntries → stripThinkingSignaturesFromEntries` code path, which is the exact persistence boundary where stale signatures were previously written.

---

*This PR was developed with AI assistance (Claude). The contributor understands the code and has verified the approach.*

target.title: fix: prevent silent message loss from EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError
target.body:
## Summary

When the OpenAI SDK retried an `ECONNRESET` internally (default `maxRetries=2`), the session write lock was released during the retry window. Incoming steering messages could then modify the transcript without updating the fence, causing a fingerprint mismatch on `reacquireAfterPrompt()`. The resulting `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` was caught by `model-fallback.ts` which aborted the run, and the outer reply pipeline produced no user-visible output — the message was silently lost.

## Root Cause Chain

```
API ECONNRESET
  → OpenAI SDK default maxRetries=2, internal retry
  → session write lock released during retry window
  → incoming message arrives via queueHandle.queueMessage
  → activeSession.steer writes directly (bypassing withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites)
  → transcript changes without updating fence
  → original attempt reacquireAfterPrompt() fingerprint mismatch
  → EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError thrown
  → model-fallback.ts aborts the run
  → outer layer hits generic failure branch
  → assistantTexts/payloads never enter buildReplyPayloads()
  → message silently lost (user sees generic error or nothing)
```

## Fix

Five coordinated changes that address the bug at multiple layers:

### 1. Wrap steering in `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites` (`attempt.ts`)
Steering writes that arrive during prompt lock release now go through `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, keeping fence tracking consistent and preventing the fingerprint mismatch that triggers the takeover error.

### 2. Dedicated error handler for takeover (`agent-runner-execution.ts`, `failover-error.ts`)
Added `isEmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError()` export and a dedicated catch block in `runAgentTurnWithFallback`. Instead of falling into the generic failure branch (silent loss), users now receive a clear message asking them to resend. When a takeover wraps a preserved prompt error (billing, rate-limit, context-overflow), the original error is unwrapped and classified normally — only pure takeover errors return resend guidance.

### 3. Scope SDK retry disable to embedded prompt lock path (`attempt.ts`, `attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Extracted `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` helper that wraps the session's `streamFn` with `{ maxRetries: 0, ...options }` — the `0` is a **default** that goes before the caller options spread, so `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` (surfaced via `options.maxRetries` per the chain in `sdk.ts:360`) takes precedence when explicitly configured. Non-embedded callers retain the SDK default retry behavior (maxRetries=2).

### 4. Forward maxRetries through native OpenAI transport (`openai-transport-stream.ts`)
`buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` previously accepted only `model` and `signal`, dropping the `maxRetries` injected by `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault`. Added `maxRetries` parameter and forwarded it through all three native transport call sites (Responses, Azure Responses, Completions). When `maxRetries` is the only option present, the function now returns an object instead of `undefined` so the SDK receives the override.

### 5. Phase tracking in `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` (`attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Added a `phase` field (`prompt_reacquire`, `cleanup`, `write`, `fence_check`) for better diagnostics when this error does occur. Backward compatible — the phase parameter is optional with existing call sites unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Silent message loss when SDK retries race with session takeover → visible resend guidance for pure takeover, correct error classification for wrapped prompt errors, provider retry settings respected, maxRetries forwarded through native OpenAI transport
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`, Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64) running patched checkout at commit `80bbe803e2`
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` only accepts `model` and `signal` — it drops `maxRetries`. Embedded prompt lock window injects `maxRetries: 0` but the native OpenAI transport never forwards it to the SDK, so SDK retries (default `maxRetries=2`) still occur during the released lock window, widening the race. Additionally, `queueMessage` steering writes bypass `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, causing fingerprint mismatch → `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` → generic failure branch → silent message loss.
- **Exact steps and commands run after this patch**:

```
$ docker run --rm -e CI=true -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app node:24-bookworm bash /proof.sh
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (v5) ===
Environment: Docker container, v24.16.0, aarch64, "Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
Commit: 80bbe803e2

--- Installing dependencies (pnpm) ---
install exit: 0
Done in 47s using pnpm v11.2.2

--- Test 1: Native OpenAI transport forwards maxRetries ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  241 passed (241)

--- Test 2: Embedded prompt lock defaults + override ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  48 passed (48)

--- Test 3: Takeover error handling + unwrapping (agent-runner-execution) ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  184 passed (184)

--- Test 4: failover-error classification ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  83 passed (83)

--- Test 5: SDK transport paths (openai-completions + openai-responses-shared) ---
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
 Tests  32 passed (32)

--- Test 6: Code path verification ---
buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions accepts+forwards maxRetries:
1843:  maxRetries?: number,
1844:): { signal?: AbortSignal; timeout?: number; maxRetries?: number } | undefined {
1846:  if (timeout === undefined && !signal && maxRetries === undefined) {
1852:    ...(maxRetries !== undefined ? { maxRetries } : {}),
1940:          options?.maxRetries,
2380:          options?.maxRetries,
2614:          buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions(model, options?.signal, options?.maxRetries),

Embedded retry default (maxRetries:0 BEFORE options spread):
1083:    return innerStreamFn(model, context, { maxRetries: 0, ...requestOptions });
```

- **Evidence after fix**: All 588 tests pass across the 6 affected test suites in a clean Docker environment.
  - **Transport retry suppression proven end-to-end**: A loopback regression test in `openai-transport-stream.test.ts` starts a mock server returning retryable HTTP 500 errors, passes `maxRetries: 0` through the full native completions transport path, and asserts exactly 1 request is received — confirming the SDK does not retry when `maxRetries: 0` is forwarded through the native transport.
  - Default SDK retry behavior preserved for non-embedded callers (no `maxRetries` in default request options).
  - Embedded prompt lock window correctly injects `maxRetries: 0` as a default that configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` can override.
  - Native OpenAI transport (`buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions`) correctly forwards `maxRetries` to all 3 SDK call sites.
  - Pure takeover errors produce visible resend guidance. Wrapped prompt errors are unwrapped and classified normally.
- **What was not tested**: No live multi-user concurrent session test with an actual ECONNRESET during an embedded prompt with concurrent steering input was run. The proof relies on unit tests (including an end-to-end loopback transport test with a real HTTP server) that simulate the session-lock race, fingerprint mismatch, transport retry suppression, and error classification paths in a real Docker container environment.

## Tests

All affected test suites pass (Docker `node:24-bookworm`, aarch64):

| Test File | Tests |
|-----------|-------|
| `openai-transport-stream.test.ts` | 241 |
| `attempt.session-lock.test.ts` | 48 |
| `agent-runner-execution.test.ts` | 184 |
| `failover-error.test.ts` | 83 |
| `openai-completions.test.ts` + `openai-responses-shared.test.ts` | 32 |

New test coverage includes:
- `buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` forwards `maxRetries` when provided, omits when undefined
- `maxRetries`-only call returns an object (not `undefined`)
- Loopback end-to-end test: mock 500 server + `maxRetries: 0` → exactly 1 request (no SDK retry)
- `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` default behavior (no caller options → maxRetries:0)
- Explicit `maxRetries` override preserved over embedded default
- Takeover detection during owned steering writes
- User-facing resend message for pure session takeover
- Preserved prompt error unwrapping from cleanup takeover
- Backward compatibility for `assertSessionFileFence` without phase parameter

## Changed Files

- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts`
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts`
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`
- `src/agents/failover-error.ts`
- `src/agents/failover-error.test.ts`
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`
- `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts`
- `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts`
- `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.ts`
- `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts`

Fixes #87180
